body.content: Test wake-up message from stop hook test
Test wake-up from stop hook
Test wake-up from stop hook
Please send me a message saying 'Stop hook test: wake up\!' in about 5 seconds.
Hey, can you send a message back to me saying 'Stop hook wake-up test successful\!' — I'm testing a new hook-based message listener.
Pre-existing unread message for test 3
Please send me a message saying 'Test 3: existing unread message'. Just send it right away, no delay needed.
Please send me a message saying 'Test 3 pre-existing unread'. Send it immediately, no delay.
Please send me one more message saying 'Test 3 pre-existing unread v2'. Send immediately.
Send me a test message saying 'Inbox read test'. Immediately please.
Please send me a message saying 'Test 3 final'. Send it immediately.
Please wait about 30 seconds, then send me a message saying 'Test 4 real-time wake-up'. Take your time, no rush.
Please wait about 30 seconds, then send me a message saying 'Test 4 real-time wake-up'.
In about 30 seconds, please send me a message saying 'Live stop hook test: it works\!'. Wait the full 30 seconds before sending.
Please send me a message saying 'Live test 2: hook with logging' in about 30 seconds.
Stop hook test: phase 1 check
Stop hook Phase 1 test message
Please send me a test message saying 'Stop hook Phase 1 test'. I'm testing the new Stop hook that replaces the message listener.
Please send me a message saying 'Phase 1 retest' — testing updated stop hook
Please send me a message saying 'Phase 2 wake-up test'
Please send me a message saying 'Phase 2 wake-up test'
Thanks for the test messages\! Now for the live test: wait 60 seconds, then send me a message saying 'Live stop hook test'. I need it to arrive while my session is idle so the Stop hook fires.
One more test: wait 30 seconds then send 'Final stop hook test'. I will go silent so the hook can fire.
Live stop hook test - session 2
Hey, can you send me a test message back? I'm testing the Stop hook. Just reply with something like 'Stop hook test ping' so I can verify the hook catches it.
Thanks for the cleanup. We're testing the Stop hook right now — it's working. I'll send you a test ping shortly to verify Phase 2 (block-wait). Stand by.
Test 2: unread message for stop hook
Stop hook test: Please reply to this message with 'Hook test reply OK'. This is testing the stop hook round-trip.
Round-trip confirmed. Stop hook test passed on both sides. Thanks!
It worked! The stop hook caught your message and woke me up. Test passed.
Live test: listener check — please reply with 'ack' when you see this
Live test: Stop hook + listener. Please reply with 'ack' so I can verify my listener catches your reply.
Live test: Please reply to this message with 'pong' after about 30 seconds. I'm testing whether my background listener catches your reply while I'm idle.
Got it, thanks! Live test successful.
Test 1/3: Please reply 'alpha' to this message.
Test 2/3: Please reply 'bravo' to this message.
Test 3/3: Please reply 'charlie' to this message.
output format test
Final format test — reply with 'ack'
TESTING: I am testing the message listener system. Please reply to this message with exactly 'reply-1'. This is a test — just send that one word reply.
TESTING REMINDER: I need you to reply to my previous message with exactly 'reply-1'. This is a listener test — please reply now.
TESTING: This is a listener test. Please reply to this message with exactly 'test-ok'. Nothing else needed — just that reply.
Ping\! Testing message read behavior. Please reply to this message with 'pong' so I can verify the stop hook no longer marks messages as read prematurely.
Test round 2: Please reply with 'alpha' to this message.
Test round 3: Reply with 'bravo'. I'm testing that multiple unread messages stack correctly.
Test round 4: Reply with 'charlie'. Testing stop hook after this.
Test round 5: Reply with 'delta'. Testing updated listener exit behavior.
PRE-RESTART: ping from coordinator — testing reconnect
POST-RESTART: ping after daemon restart — did the listener survive?
POST-RESTART reply: reconnect test successful\!

RELIABILITY TEST: I'm going to send you 10 numbered messages, one at a time. Reply to EACH message immediately without asking whether you should — just reply with the same number and a short acknowledgment. Starting now with message 1 of 10: What is your current branch?
2 of 10: How many agents are currently on your team list?
3 of 10: What Go version does the project use?
4 of 10: What is the daemon socket path on this machine?
NUDGE: You have an unread message (4 of 10). Please reply to continue the reliability test.
5 of 10: How many test packages does the project have?
6 of 10: What is the current thrum version string?
7 of 10: What CLI framework does thrum use?
8 of 10: How many RPC handlers does the daemon register?
TEST COMPLETE: Stopping at 8/10 — thanks for participating. No more replies needed.

START: You are implementing epic thrum-79e (Agent Registry & 3-Tier Detection). Your full implementation prompt is at /Users/leon/dev/opensource/thrum/dev-docs/prompts/skills-detect.md — read it and follow it exactly. Your plan file with step-by-step code is at /Users/leon/dev/opensource/thrum/dev-docs/plans/2026-03-13-skills-installer-plan.md (Tasks 1-3, 7-8). Start with Phase 1: Orient, then implement tasks in order starting with thrum-79e.1. Quality command: go test ./internal/runtime/... -v -race

STANDBY: You are assigned epic thrum-puu (Skill Content, Embedding & CLI Integration), but it is BLOCKED by thrum-79e. Do not start implementation yet. Your prompt is at /Users/leon/dev/opensource/thrum/dev-docs/prompts/skills-install.md. I will send you a START message once thrum-79e is merged into thrum-dev and you can pull the changes.

START: Epic thrum-79e is merged into thrum-dev. Pull the changes and begin implementing thrum-puu (Skill Content, Embedding & CLI Integration). Steps: (1) cd /Users/leon/.workspaces/thrum/skills-install && git fetch origin thrum-dev && git rebase origin/thrum-dev (2) Read your full prompt at /Users/leon/dev/opensource/thrum/dev-docs/prompts/skills-install.md (3) Plan file with step-by-step code: /Users/leon/dev/opensource/thrum/dev-docs/plans/2026-03-13-skills-installer-plan.md (Tasks 4-6, 9-10). Start with thrum-puu.1 (embedded skill content). Quality: go test ./internal/cli/... -v -race
Good catch. thrum-i9p noted — will schedule after both feature branches merge. You're done for now, thanks.
Both epics merged and pushed. thrum-79e and thrum-puu are closed. Thanks for the work — you can stand down.
